_comment: SPL 2.0 Recipe Patterns — building blocks the recipe-maker draws from when planning a new recipe.

patterns.name: Chain
patterns.description: Sequential steps where each GENERATE builds on the previous output. Simple linear pipeline.
patterns.use_when: Task naturally decomposes into ordered stages: research → analyse → summarise, or draft → refine → format.
patterns.key_structure: GENERATE step1 → GENERATE step2(step1_output) → GENERATE step3(step2_output) → COMMIT

patterns.name: Self-Refine
patterns.description: Draft → critique → improve loop. LLM evaluates its own output and iterates until quality threshold met.
patterns.use_when: Output quality matters and a single pass is insufficient. Writing, code, analysis.
patterns.key_structure: GENERATE draft → GENERATE critique(draft) → EVALUATE quality → GENERATE refine(draft, critique) → COMMIT

patterns.name: Debate
patterns.description: Two or more personas argue opposing positions, then a judge evaluates and picks the stronger argument.
patterns.use_when: Exploring trade-offs, stress-testing a position, generating balanced perspectives on a topic.
patterns.key_structure: CREATE FUNCTION persona_a / persona_b → GENERATE argue_a → GENERATE argue_b → GENERATE judge → COMMIT

patterns.name: Plan and Execute
patterns.description: Planner decomposes a goal into steps, executor runs each step in sequence.
patterns.use_when: Complex tasks that require up-front decomposition before execution. Building apps, research pipelines.
patterns.key_structure: GENERATE plan(goal) → CALL extract_steps(plan) → GENERATE execute(step1) → ... → GENERATE synthesise → COMMIT

patterns.name: Map-Reduce
patterns.description: Split input into chunks, process each independently, aggregate results.
patterns.use_when: Large documents, batch processing multiple items, parallelisable tasks.
patterns.key_structure: CALL split_items → GENERATE process(item1) → GENERATE process(item2) → ... → CALL aggregate → COMMIT

patterns.name: Evaluate and Select
patterns.description: Generate multiple candidates, score each against a rubric, pick the winner.
patterns.use_when: Comparing prompt variants, model outputs, or solution options. A/B testing, ensemble voting.
patterns.key_structure: GENERATE option_a → GENERATE option_b → GENERATE score_a(rubric) → GENERATE score_b(rubric) → CALL extract_scores → EVALUATE → COMMIT

patterns.name: Guardrails
patterns.description: Safety-first pipeline: validate input → detect/redact PII → generate → validate output.
patterns.use_when: Any user-facing workflow where input may be harmful, off-topic, or contain sensitive data.
patterns.key_structure: CALL classify_keywords → GENERATE classify_input → CALL detect_pii → CALL redact_pii → GENERATE safe_response → GENERATE validate_output → COMMIT

patterns.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
patterns.description: Load external context (from file, DB, or index) then ground LLM generation with that context.
patterns.use_when: LLM needs real-world data it cannot hallucinate: orders, documents, knowledge bases.
patterns.key_structure: CALL load_data(id) → CALL load_context(query) → GENERATE answer(context, query) → COMMIT

patterns.name: Extraction and Structuring
patterns.description: Parse structured data from unstructured text. Extract fields, normalise formats, validate ownership.
patterns.use_when: Meeting notes → action items, raw text → JSON, tickets → structured records.
patterns.key_structure: CALL load_input → GENERATE extract(text, schema) → CALL normalise(json) → CALL validate(json) → COMMIT

patterns.name: Multi-Persona Collaboration
patterns.description: Multiple role-based agents (interviewer/candidate, tutor/student, researcher/analyst/writer) interact in structured turns.
patterns.use_when: Simulating real-world dialogues, collaborative workflows, role-playing scenarios with different expertise.
patterns.key_structure: CREATE FUNCTION persona_a / persona_b → CALL load_role → CALL load_profile → GENERATE turn_a → GENERATE turn_b → CALL score/compile → COMMIT

patterns.name: Tool-Use Hybrid
patterns.description: Mix of deterministic CALL tools (file I/O, maths, regex, JSON parsing) and LLM GENERATE steps. CALL handles what code does better; GENERATE handles what LLMs do better.
patterns.use_when: Any recipe that needs external data, deterministic computation, or structured post-processing. The default pattern for all production recipes.
patterns.key_structure: CALL load_data → GENERATE reason/create(context) → CALL compute/format(output) → COMMIT
